enonce.texte: Read each definition carefully and choose the correct banking term from the options. This exercise tests your knowledge of key vocabulary from Lesson 39.

questions.texte: Identify the term: 'The money you put into a bank account.'

sousQuestions.texte:
A) Withdraw
B) Deposit
C) Loan
D) Interest

questions.texte: Identify the term: 'An amount of money you borrow from the bank, which you must pay back.'

sousQuestions.texte:
A) Debit
B) Credit
C) Loan
D) Account

questions.texte: Identify the term: 'The extra money you pay to the bank for borrowing money, usually a percentage.'

sousQuestions.texte:
A) Withdrawal
B) Interest
C) Transfer
D) ATM fee

questions.texte: Identify the term: 'A machine that lets you take money out of your account 24/7.'

sousQuestions.texte:
A) Bank counter
B) Credit card
C) ATM
D) Chequebook

questions.texte: Identify the term: 'A card that takes money directly from your bank account when you pay.'

sousQuestions.texte:
A) Credit card
B) Debit card
C) Identity card
D) SIM card

enonce.texte: You are a young entrepreneur in Douala. You want to save money from your small business (selling phone accessories) and open a bank account. Read the conversation and answer the questions.
images: A simple illustration of a bank teller counter in Cameroon with a customer holding money and a mobile phone

questions.texte: Distinguish the two main types of bank accounts the teller mentions.

sousQuestions.texte: Based on the dialogue, what is the main difference between them?
sousQuestions.reponse: A savings account is for saving money and earning interest, while a current account is for daily transactions (paying bills, receiving money).

questions.texte: Apply your knowledge: You want to send 10,000 FCFA to your mother who lives in another city. Which method from the lesson would be the safest and fastest?

sousQuestions.texte:
A) Withdraw cash and send it by bus
B) Bank transfer via mobile banking
C) Give the money to a friend
D) Keep the money in your wallet
sousQuestions.reponse: B) Bank transfer via mobile banking

questions.texte: Analyze this situation: Your account balance is 25,000 FCFA. You try to withdraw 30,000 FCFA from an ATM. What will happen and why?
questions.reponse: The transaction will be declined because of insufficient funds.

sousQuestions.texte:
A) The ATM will give you 25,000 FCFA
B) The transaction will be declined
C) The bank will give you a loan automatically
D) You will pay a lower interest
sousQuestions.reponse: B) The transaction will be declined

enonce.texte:
You have just received this bank receipt after a transaction. Use the details to answer the questions.

Transaction Receipt:
Date: 15/10/2025
Transaction Type: Withdrawal
Amount: 50,000 FCFA
Account Balance (after): 75,000 FCFA
ATM Fee: 500 FCFA
Location: Buea Branch

questions.texte: Calculate the account balance BEFORE this withdrawal.

sousQuestions.reponse: Balance after (75,000) + Withdrawal amount (50,000) = 125,000 FCFA. (The ATM fee is already included in the 50,000 or deducted separately, but the simplest logic: before withdrawal, he had 125,000 FCFA).

questions.texte: Evaluate the bank's policy: The receipt shows a 500 FCFA ATM fee. If you think this is too expensive for students, what advice would you give to a friend to avoid this fee?
questions.reponse: Use the bank counter during working hours or use your bank's own ATMs instead of other banks' ATMs.

sousQuestions.texte:
A) Stop using banks completely
B) Only withdraw money from the bank teller inside the branch
C) Complain loudly at the ATM
D) Ask for a loan to cover the fees
sousQuestions.reponse: B) Only withdraw money from the bank teller inside the branch

questions.texte: Create a simple budget plan. You want to save 20,000 FCFA per month. List two actions you would take using banking services to achieve this goal.
questions.reponse: 1. Set up an automatic transfer (standing order) from my current account to my savings account every month. 2. Avoid unnecessary ATM fees by planning withdrawals.

sousQuestions.reponse: (Student's answer may vary). Example: 'I will open a savings account and ask the bank to automatically transfer 20,000 FCFA on payday, and I will not use my debit card for small purchases.'
